description: Endpoint-mapped Day 10 quality evaluation dataset with 10 fresh cases per endpoint.

payload.issue: Quarterly access review found five terminated contractors still assigned privileged database roles in the finance reporting environment.
SOX evidence for production change approvals is missing for emergency patches deployed during the last close period.
A public-facing Ubuntu server is running an unsupported OpenSSL package with known high severity vulnerabilities.
The incident response runbook does not define escalation contacts, evidence retention steps, or post-incident review ownership.
Security monitoring detected repeated failed administrator login attempts followed by a successful login from an unrecognized country.
Audit logs show customer export files stored in a shared object bucket without encryption or lifecycle retention rules.
Nightly database backups for the ticketing platform have failed for six consecutive days without alert acknowledgement.
Network scan identified internal management ports exposed to the corporate user VLAN without firewall restrictions.
Developers are using shared local administrator credentials on build servers contrary to privileged access policy.
The password reset workflow does not enforce multi-factor verification for high-risk account recovery requests.
Role mapping for the procurement application grants invoice approval rights to users who should only submit purchase requests.
PCI control testing found that quarterly vulnerability scan exceptions were approved without documented business justification.
The VPN gateway firmware is two major versions behind and lacks vendor fixes for active exploitation vulnerabilities.
The data retention standard references retired storage systems and does not include ownership for archive deletion reviews.
Endpoint protection quarantined malware on three laptops, but incident tickets do not show containment verification or lessons learned.
A business intelligence dashboard exposes salary fields to the entire operations group instead of the approved HR audience.
Restore testing for the document management database has not been completed during the current fiscal year.
Kubernetes nodes allow unrestricted outbound internet access from workloads that process regulated audit evidence.
Production support staff are closing change tickets without attaching implementation evidence or peer review notes.
Cloud storage buckets used by audit exports do not enforce server-side encryption or public access blocking controls.
The identity provider synchronization job failed, leaving disabled users active in downstream SaaS applications for two weeks.
GDPR data subject deletion requests are not tracked to completion across analytics replicas and reporting marts.
Several internal Jenkins plugins are no longer maintained and lack a documented upgrade or replacement plan.
The vendor access procedure does not define approval expiry, access review cadence, or emergency revocation steps.
A privileged API token was used outside normal deployment windows, but the investigation did not document token rotation.
Debug logging in the customer support service records full request payloads for failed payment troubleshooting sessions.
The disaster recovery plan lists backup replication as active, but storage metrics show replication lag exceeding seven days.
Administrative SSH access to Linux servers is allowed from broad office subnets instead of a hardened bastion host.
Monthly security awareness attestations are incomplete for a subset of temporary workers assigned to audit operations.
The audit issue workflow permits closure without requiring remediation evidence, verification notes, or owner approval.
